commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7426916f2ad2a226ac490347b48c4f28f08120fe)
commands.review_summary:
This PR strips leading slash/backslash prefixes before Windows scheduled-task name matching in `findExtraGatewayServices` and adds a mocked regression for a backslashed canonical task plus a legacy task.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +27. Total +27 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main skips `OpenClaw Gateway` but not the `\OpenClaw Gateway` form shown by Windows `schtasks`, so that task can flow into the extra-service warning path. I did not run a live Windows Task Scheduler repro from this read-only Linux checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human PR handling is needed because the branch has a concrete matcher defect and the contributor still owes real Windows proof; repair automation should not run while proof is missing.

target.title: fix: ignore canonical Windows gateway task names
target.body:
## Summary

Stop the Windows gateway-service detector from flagging the canonical `\OpenClaw Gateway` scheduled task as an extra service just because `schtasks` prefixes the task name with a backslash.

## Changes

- strip leading slash and backslash prefixes before canonical scheduled-task name matching
- preserve legacy-task detection for genuinely different task names
- add a focused regression test covering `TaskName: \OpenClaw Gateway` alongside a legacy task in the same `schtasks` output

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/inspect.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.print.test.ts`

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#90494
target.author: arkyu2077
